verdict_reason: Chapter 2 thesis: refuse the tactical answer, reframe at first principles, three threads (Sāṅkhya / karma-yoga / sthita-prajña). 2.45 sits at the intersection of karma-yoga and sthita-prajña — it issues the operative imperative for the cognitive-state shift that the karma-yoga discipline requires (nistrai-guṇya) and names the qualifications of the established operator (nirdvandva, niryoga-kṣema, ātmavān) which preview the sthita-prajña description starting at 2.54. The engineering layer preserves this dual anchoring. Verse ordering: 2.41 (precondition) → 2.42-43 (obstruction) → 2.44 (consequence) → 2.45 (operative imperative + qualifications). 2.45 closes the buddhi-yoga gateway block. The block builds correctly, and 2.45 is the natural close. PASS for both 10.3 and 10.4.
